title: cynoglossum pustulatum subsp. pustulatum
intro: Cynoglossum pustulatum subsp. pustulatum, also known as the Bluebells or the Chinese Hound's Tongue, is a plant species within the Boraginaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and cold, and treating respiratory issues, as well as its use in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments. The herbal preparations of Cynoglossum pustulatum subsp. pustulatum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
